This week on the What The Hell Is Going On podcast, @DPletka and @MarcThiessen are joined by @crmiller1 to discuss the security threat posed by Chinese electric vehicles. 

Tune in below to learn more.
podcasts.apple.com/us/podcas…

We can now measure whether graduates succeed in the labor market—what they earn and whether their education paid off. Now that outcomes can be observed directly, crude proxies like instructional time should become passé, writes AEI’s @DrBethAkers
realcleareducation.com/artic…

Israelis have managed to endure even the latest unpleasantness with characteristic resilience. Life goes on as much as possible, given the circumstances, writes AEI’s Michael Rosen.

washingtonexaminer.com/premi…

Wondering what's the latest in Iran?

Tune in one hour from now for a conversation with @criticalthreats fellows @NicholasACarl and @brian_cartr on the latest updates and analysis of what comes next. nitter.net/i/spaces/1qKDzPMzmeOJV…

If we want to understand what the job of teaching has become, those who decided they could no longer do it might be among our most useful witnesses, writes AEI’s @rpondiscio

aei.org/education/listening-…

AEI’s @DAlexBlumenthal: I think we really have to compartmentalize the visit from Beijing’s disagreement with the United States on Iran. Xi Jinping really wants the summit to succeed.

Video

Legislators should not worry that age limits will detract from parents’ indispensable role in raising children. As the polls indicate, what parents actually want is help in protecting their kids from the designs of the tech companies, writes AEI’s @RameshPonnuru

washingtonpost.com/opinions/…

Wondering what's next in Iran?

Join tomorrow for a live conversation with AEI @criticalthreats fellows @NicholasACarl and @brian_cartr on what comes next for the Middle East. 

nitter.net/i/spaces/1qKDzPMzmeOJV

If Congress wants to help housing markets, it should focus on policies that allow more homes to be built and remove barriers that restrict supply, writes AEI’s @TobiasPeterAEI aei.org/articles/by-targetin…

We may think of public housing as a big city program, but it’s found across America, including in small towns in the Deep South.

If local officials decide that a time limit would lead to families being left on the street, that will be their choice. What makes sense in Birmingham won’t make sense for Brooklyn, writes AEI’s Howard Husock.

washingtonexaminer.com/resto…
